title: alopecurus vaginatus subsp. glacialis
intro: Alopecurus vaginatus subsp. glacialis, also known as Glacier Foxtail, is a subspecies of the Foxtail grass, a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Alopecurus vaginatus subsp. glacialis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas and infusions, often made by steeping the dried roots or leaves in hot water, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
